mechanism_narrative: Persistently low federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive return frequency drops below 50% during low-rate periods.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending power, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in > 65% of triggered instances or the signal loses statistical significance with p > 0.10 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in real sales of automobiles and light trucks alongside stable consumer price indices indicates robust consumer demand without inflationary pressure, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when CPIAUCSL growth exceeds 2.5% annually, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in the index of industrial production reflect expanding manufacturing output and supply-side strength, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: The signal reverses direction during supply-constrained regimes or produces negative returns in > 55% of positive INDPRO months.

mechanism_narrative: A year-over-year decline in housing starts exceeding 10 percent signals collapsing residential investment and broader demand weakness, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in > 60% of triggered cases or the housing signal loses predictive power when mortgage rates fall below 4%.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in total nonfarm payrolls and decreases in the unemployment rate confirm broad-based labor market improvement, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to outperform a baseline model when wage growth contradicts payroll trends, or the interaction effect vanishes in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rates exceeding 2.0 percent trigger immediate equity repricing as higher discount rates compress valuation multiples, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: The signal fails to predict negative returns when inflation expectations remain anchored below 2%, or positive return frequency exceeds 55% during high-rate periods.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real gross domestic product reflects expanding economic activity and corporate revenue potential, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.01 over rolling 3-year windows or the signal consistently underperforms during periods of financial stress.
